column33: Queen Storm and Karnak for the win.

Soooo many SHIELD officers and not enough KO. First attempt with Wiccan was a flop, went with a stronger shard generator in 2nd attempt, where Adam Warlock and Quasar (blond cosmic shard bois) were one deck and Medusa and Okoye had great synergy for the other. Neither deck really wanted the wedding party cards, but they didn’t get in the way much. Highlights were triggering Crystal’s common au natural to get enough attack to clear the SHIELD villains and get a Maria hit. Feeling good this week but we’ll see. Felt just beating Maria with no escapes was an accomplishment.

column33: Not a fan of Maria Hill. Feeling like this is the week I'm eliminated.

OMG So many Marias... With my partner getting "How do you solve a problem like Maria?" stuck in our heads throughout!

Our first game we tried to use Mar-vell for shards and Ronan for bystander rescuing. It went really terrible with a clogged HQ and worse clogged decks. Took a chance to mix it up and hoped for Tigra's KO ability combined with Phyla-Vell's yellow to help activate it. I think we only managed to KO 2 cards with it ... but got lucky with Quasar's rare early to help us keep the city under control. 
Felt like it still took too long on turns and was frustrating to see the higher VP value villains never came out.

Maria Hill is such a pain and that one tactic that puts officers into the city is brutal! But overall my strategy was to gain shards and stash 7 of the. Away until I got the right opportunity. Worked out great!

I haven't played Maria Hill in forever (as she's so tough to defeat), but having the use of shards and utilizing Nova and Phayla-Vell to create a shard making machine helped out. I still felt the set up was tough but not unfair. Super fun!!!
Sneaky setup. Invisible woman was a last second choice for the bystander covering. Zombie melk tastes a little funky…

Brought Ultimate Spider Man to assist Symbiote Spider Man, and Invisible Woman for her uncommon, as it was a decent way to bury the zombies.

SBPKECN went with the Spider deck, but did recruit a copy of Invisible Woman's uncommon, to keep her dead buried. In addition, she also picked up Invisible Woman's rare(just before Gobby would have killed it). That took even more pressure off, since all the villains had ambushes on them, that she could cancel, resulting in several 10 card hands. 

Snash, unfortunately just kept killing the same Ghost Racer, over and over again for 1 measely point...

My first game had too many escapes,  so I selected heroes to stop rise of the living dead. The reality gem helped bury some Masterstrikes. I  had a bagel shuffle with 2 scheme twists and 1 Masterstrike in the first 4 turns. I was able to get Invisible woman rare on turn 5 and this made most ambush abilities worthless.
Better to be lucky than skilled this game. I also used disappearing act to ko some gems so they couldn't go back into the city.
The living dead suck. The first useful Spider-Man game.

Heroes choice was not a choice. The first attempt i resigned, since there were no uncommons i wanted in hq. The second try went a bit better, however turn 1 ms koing a rare was not nice. Hand1 finally managed to get a solid hand while most greys were in discard and koed 6 of them. Was cleanup after that.

Very rough setup. It was fun trying to navigate the villains to try and limit what comes back from the victory piles. The spider-men do what spider-men do. Game 1 we tried old man Logan to combo with torch and gamora but it didn't work out well. We actually won that game faster but had more escapes. Game 2 we tried invisible woman and got lucky to be able to use her focus to cover up living dead villains with bystanders. We did take a bit longer and had a slightly lower PPT but figured it was better to only have the 1 escape and take a little longer. Overall it was a fun setup that took some out of the box thinking and brought a good challenge

We got much luckier on our deck shuffle the second time. Ultimate Spider-Mans rare was super over powered. Both times the player that went with the spider man deck ended up just killing everything off and there was no vp left for the other player.

Spider-man seemed to always be in the way. We were able to wall-crawl him to our hand to get some extra strength heroes for Marvelous Strength card. Other than that he was a cheap scratch card to KO when fighting the henchmen. We had a hand that took anything to cycle the HQ and one hand that was pure Captain Marvel and Lady Thor.

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33:
Reality, Soul, Time, Space.

Only got one attempt in this week. Not enough time for the second. But what a game it was!
On turn 1 I acquired my first Unseen Rescue. And the left hand from there was an absolute burying machine. I got another UR and some Four of a Kinds and tore through the deadlands. I never got a Disappearing Act but with some lucky radiation bystanders and shots at undead Hydra and MODOK I whittled the deck down and every other turn I was slamming. Left focused on keeping the city clear of the baddies and burying them so it stayed barren. 
So who was the star of the right hand? The freaking soul gem. Right hand began as a Symbiote Spider-Man deck, a simple dream. Turns unfolded and one, two gems were acquired. Reality and Space were obtained early. They bounced in and out until the right acquired the soul gem. Then things got interesting as we started defeating ghost racers and stacked up shards on the gem. The picture became clearer. 
Schemes occurred and Right had to drop gems but never had to drop the SG which allowed me to quickly defeat the other gem entering the city, and keep building its shard count. End of game I had 7 shards on the soul gem, meaning I had at least 7 attack every turn in the "weaker" deck. 
We hit turn 40, had one card left in the VD and it was right hands turn and I laid down the time gem, making three total, one to go until the task was complete. Switching to left hand would mean defeat as Gobs had two tactics left and the final blow, there was no way we could muster that amount of attack. There was only one way to win. I activated the time gem, and drew my next right hand and lo' and behold the space gem was among the six I drew. I activated my fourth gem after the last ghost racer moved from the VD into the city. Do your worst racer, for I am inevitable The gauntlet was forged. The primarily spidey-deck (filled with wounds because of human torch) had acquired the four gems listed at the top of this comment, and the Zombified corpse of Norman Osborn was turned to ash.
Overall a good time, and got to use some heroes I hadn't used yet, so that's always fun

column33: We decided to go with MCU Black Panther to really focus on the multiclass cards and Empower for player 2 and MCU Dr Strange and Dr Strange Supreme for the other player. Black Panther and T'Challa struggled to get going and had a bloated deck that caused trouble getting good turns. The Dr. S combo however was great! Supreme's Break an Absolute Point cleared my deck and let me ensure What If... activated. Soulbind made it so i could permanantly bury the Zombies and because they were powerful MM level villians, Supreme's rare let me basically draw a whole extra hand when I played it. I was able to get my deck thin enough to draw my full 15ish card deck 3 turns in a row and hit Thanos multiple times each turn. Super fun game and glad I got to use Supreme Dr Strange the way he was intended.

column33: Stupid Iron Spider

This one was another fun one, but I don't like schemes where it is just get unlucky and lose after x twists.  Regardless, First run I had a really nice black widow deck.  Next one I managed to get a great tech focus deck, using the recruit to buy 3/5 of the rares (black widow, ironman, hulk) and got the win with lots of carddraw and big power.  What more could you ask for?
